metadata.title: Guidance for Clinical Trial Sponsors: Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees
metadata.note: This PDF contains the FDA guidance on Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees (DMCs/DSMBs), March 2006. The filename 37-safety-reporting-inds.pdf does not match the document content.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors of studies evaluating new drugs, biologics, and devices are required to monitor these studies in accordance with applicable regulations.
rules.source_section: Section 1 - Introduction and Background
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors of studies evaluating new drugs, biologics, and devices are required to monitor these studies (see 21 CFR 312.50 and 312.56 for drugs and biologics, and 21 CFR 812.40 and 21 CFR 812.46 for devices).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should consider establishing a Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) when the study endpoint is such that a highly favorable or unfavorable result, or a finding of futility, at an interim analysis might ethically require termination of the study before its planned completion.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - What is the Risk to Trial Participants?
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors consider using a DMC when: The study endpoint is such that a highly favorable or unfavorable result, or even a finding of futility, at an interim analysis might ethically require termination of the study before its planned completion.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should consider establishing a DMC when there are a priori reasons for a particular safety concern, when the procedure for administering the treatment is particularly invasive, or when there is prior information suggesting serious toxicity with the study treatment.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - What is the Risk to Trial Participants?
rules.verbatim_quote: There are a priori reasons for a particular safety concern, as, for example, if the procedure for administering the treatment is particularly invasive; There is prior information suggesting the possibility of serious toxicity with the study treatment.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should consider establishing a DMC when the study is being performed in a potentially fragile population such as children, pregnant women, the very elderly, or other vulnerable populations.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - What is the Risk to Trial Participants?
rules.verbatim_quote: The study is being performed in a potentially fragile population such as children, pregnant women or the very elderly, or other vulnerable populations, such as those who are terminally ill or of diminished mental capacity.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors of well-controlled studies must take appropriate measures to minimize bias, including establishing written procedures to ensure the confidentiality of unblinded interim comparative data.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 - Confidentiality of Interim Data and Analyses
rules.verbatim_quote: As described in 21 CFR 314.126(b)(5) (drugs) and 21 CFR 860.7(f)(1) (devices), sponsors of well-controlled studies should take appropriate measures to minimize bias. Consistent with 21 CFR 314.126(b)(5) (drugs) and 21 CFR 860.7(f)(1) (devices), sponsors should establish written procedures, which may be included in the DMC charter, to ensure the minimization of bias, such as maintaining confidentiality of the interim data.
rules.exceptions: In open-label trials with special concerns about safety, there may be a rationale for the sponsor and/or investigators to have access to the ongoing comparative safety data to ensure continuous monitoring.

rules.rule_text: Unblinded interim data and results of comparative interim analyses should generally not be accessible by anyone other than DMC members or the statistician performing these analyses and presenting them to the DMC.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 - Confidentiality of Interim Data and Analyses
rules.verbatim_quote: Unblinded interim data and the results of comparative interim analyses, therefore, should generally not be accessible by anyone other than DMC members or the statistician(s) performing these analyses and presenting them to the DMC.
rules.exceptions: Sponsor access may be justified in open-label trials with special safety concerns; such access must be specified and justified in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: Any part of the interim report to the DMC that includes comparative effectiveness and safety data presented by study group must be available only to DMC members during the course of the trial, including any follow-up period.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Interim Reports to the DMC
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that any part of the interim report to the DMC that includes comparative effectiveness and safety data presented by study group, whether coded or completely unblinded, be available only to DMC members during the course of the trial, including any follow-up period—that is, until the trial is completed and the blind is broken for the sponsor and investigators.

rules.rule_text: DMCs must operate under a written charter that includes well-defined standard operating procedures, established before the initiation of interim analyses.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3 - Establishing a Charter Describing Standard Operating Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: DMCs typically operate under a written charter that includes well-defined standard operating procedures. Such charters are important for the same reason that study protocols and analytical plans are important—they document that procedures were pre-specified and thereby reduce concerns that operations inappropriately influenced by interim data could bias the trial results and interpretation.

rules.rule_text: FDA may request that the sponsor submit the DMC charter to FDA well in advance of the performance of any interim analyses, ideally before the initiation of the trial.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3 - Establishing a Charter Describing Standard Operating Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA may request that the sponsor submit the charter to FDA well in advance of the performance of any interim analyses, ideally before the initiation of the trial (see 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii)(g); 21 CFR 312.41(a); 21 CFR 812.150(b)(10)).

rules.rule_text: Statistical descriptions for interim analysis must be complete before the conduct of any unblinded interim analyses to minimize the potential for bias.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3.1.1 - Meeting Schedule and Format
rules.verbatim_quote: To minimize the potential for bias, these descriptions should be complete before the conduct of any unblinded interim analyses (see 21 CFR 314.126(b)(5) (drugs) and 21 CFR 860.7(f)(1) (devices)).

rules.rule_text: FDA will typically request that the sponsor submit a final monitoring plan once it has been put in place and before the initiation of interim monitoring.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3.2 - Statistical Methods
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA will typically request that the sponsor submit a final monitoring plan once it has been put in place, and before the initiation of interim monitoring, as such a plan would typically be considered a critical component of the study protocol (see 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii)(g); 21 CFR 312.41(a); 21 CFR 812.150(b)(10)).

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must establish procedures to safeguard confidential interim data from the project team, investigators, sponsor representatives, or anyone else outside the DMC and the statistician performing the interim analyses.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.1 - Interim Data
rules.verbatim_quote: Procedures should be established to safeguard confidential interim data from the project team, investigators, sponsor representatives, or anyone else outside the DMC and the statistician(s) performing the interim analyses (see 21 CFR 314.126(b)(5) (drugs) and 21 CFR 860.7(f)(1) (devices)).

rules.rule_text: DMC members must not include investigators currently entering subjects into the trial under review, to avoid conflicts of interest from knowledge of interim results influencing trial conduct.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1 - Committee Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: We therefore recommend that DMC members for a given trial not include investigators in that trial.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must establish procedures to assess potential conflicts of interest of proposed DMC members and ensure that those with serious conflicts of interest are not included on the DMC.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1 - Committee Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors establish procedures to: Assess potential conflicts of interest of proposed DMC members; Ensure that those with serious conflicts of interest are not included on the DMC; Provide disclosure to all DMC members of any potential conflicts that are not thought to impede objectivity and thus would not preclude service on the DMC; Identify and disclose any concurrent service of any DMC member on other DMCs of the same, related or competing products.

rules.rule_text: All clinical trials conducted under an IND or IDE are subject to regulatory safety reporting requirements including prompt reporting to FDA of certain serious and unexpected adverse events.
rules.source_section: Section 5 - DMC Recommendations and Regulatory Reporting Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: All clinical trials conducted under an IND or IDE are subject to regulatory safety reporting requirements. These requirements include prompt reporting to FDA of certain serious and unexpected adverse events (see 21 CFR 312.32(c), 21 CFR 312.52, 21 CFR 812.46(b), 21 CFR 812.150(b)(1)).

rules.rule_text: The sponsor is responsible for notifying FDA of serious events potentially related to administration of a medical product under 21 CFR 312.32 and 21 CFR 812.150(b)(1).
rules.source_section: Section 5 - DMC Recommendations and Regulatory Reporting Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, for an event that is individually recognizable as a serious event potentially related to administration of a medical product (e.g., agranulocytosis, hepatotoxicity for drug studies), the sponsor (sometimes through a CRO managing that aspect of the trial, see 21 CFR 312.52) is responsible for notifying FDA (21 CFR 312.32, 21 CFR 812.150(b)(1)).
rules.exceptions: The sponsor may make this notification with or without unblinding the individual case.

rules.rule_text: When a DMC recommendation conveys findings associated with serious unexpected adverse events, the sponsor must investigate and report such findings to FDA under 21 CFR 312.32.
rules.source_section: Section 5 - DMC Recommendations and Regulatory Reporting Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: As required by 21 CFR 312.32(d)(1), the sponsor would need to investigate a DMC's recommendation relating to such events as potentially reportable to FDA under 21 CFR 312.32. If the sponsor concluded that the increased rate of serious unanticipated adverse events was 'associated with the use of the drug,' the finding, and support for it (which could include the DMC report, any analysis, and pertinent data) would need to be submitted as a serious unexpected adverse experience.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must report an analysis of serious and unexpected adverse events to FDA and to all study investigators when such events are identified through DMC comparative analysis.
rules.source_section: Section 5 - DMC Recommendations and Regulatory Reporting Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: Findings conveyed to a sponsor by a DMC as part of a recommendation to modify the trial could therefore mean that serious and unexpected events were occurring, and the sponsor would consequently be required to report an analysis of these events to FDA and to all study investigators according to 21 CFR 312.32(c)(1)(B)(ii) (drug trials) and 21 CFR 812.150(b)(1) (device trials).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must inform FDA about all DMC recommendations related to the safety of the investigational product, whether or not the adverse event meets the definition of 'serious.'
rules.source_section: Section 5 - DMC Recommendations and Regulatory Reporting Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors inform FDA about all recommendations related to the safety of the investigational product whether or not the adverse event in question meets the definition of 'serious.'

rules.rule_text: For drug and biologic studies, sponsors must notify IRBs, FDA, and other investigators if the sponsor withdraws the IND for a safety reason.
rules.source_section: Section 3.5 - Others with Monitoring Responsibilities
rules.verbatim_quote: For drug and biologic studies, sponsors must notify IRBs, as well as FDA and other investigators, if the sponsor withdraws the IND for a safety reason (21 CFR 312.38(c)).

rules.rule_text: For device trials, the sponsor is responsible for ensuring that FDA and any reviewing IRBs are promptly informed of significant new information about an investigation.
rules.source_section: Section 3.5 - Others with Monitoring Responsibilities
rules.verbatim_quote: ensuring that FDA and any reviewing IRB(s) are promptly informed of significant new information about an investigation (21 CFR 812.40).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must report to participating IRBs, as well as to FDA, applicable changes in protocol or study procedures made as a result of DMC recommendations.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.3.2 - Maintaining Meeting Records
rules.verbatim_quote: Of course, sponsors and/or investigators must report to participating IRBs, as well as to FDA, applicable changes in the protocol or study procedures made as a result of DMC recommendations (see 21 CFR 56.108(a)(3) and (4) and 312.30 and 312.66 for drugs and 21 CFR 812.40 for devices).

rules.rule_text: The DMC must keep minutes of all meetings and should divide meeting minutes into two parts according to whether they include discussion of confidential data.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.3.2 - Maintaining Meeting Records
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that the DMC keep minutes of all meetings (see Guidance for Industry, ICH E6, Good Clinical Practice: Consolidated Guidance, Section 5.5 at 5.5.2). We also recommend that the DMC divide meeting minutes into two parts, according to whether they include discussion of confidential data (usually unblinded comparative data).

rules.rule_text: After each meeting, the DMC must issue a written report to the sponsor based on the meeting minutes, including sufficient information to explain the rationale for any recommended changes.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.3.2 - Maintaining Meeting Records
rules.verbatim_quote: We also recommend that after each meeting, the DMC issue a written report to the sponsor based on the meeting minutes. This report does not have to be extremely detailed, but should include sufficient information to explain the rationale for any recommended changes.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should arrange for archiving electronic data sets used for each set of interim analysis, as FDA may request access to these records when the study is completed.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.3.2 - Maintaining Meeting Records
rules.verbatim_quote: We may also request access to the electronic data sets used for each set of interim analysis. We therefore recommend that sponsors arrange for archiving such electronic data sets.

rules.rule_text: For trials that may be terminated because of safety concerns, timely communication with FDA is often required and sponsors must initiate discussion with FDA as soon as possible about the appropriate course of action.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2.1 - DMC Recommendations to Terminate the Study
rules.verbatim_quote: For trials that may be terminated because of safety concerns, timely communication with FDA is often required (see, e.g., 21 CFR 312.56(d) (drugs); 21 CFR 812.150 (devices)). In such cases, we recommend that the sponsor initiate discussion as soon as possible about the appropriate course of action, for the trial in question as well as any other use of the investigational product.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should discuss proposed protocol changes that could substantially impact the validity or regulatory acceptability of a trial with FDA before implementation, particularly when such changes might have been motivated by interim data.
rules.source_section: Section 7.3 - DMC Recommendations for Protocol Changes
rules.verbatim_quote: Certain types of changes to the protocol, however, such as changes in the primary endpoints, could have substantial impact on the validity of the trial and/or its ability to support the desired regulatory decision if they potentially could have been motivated by the interim data. We recommend that sponsors discuss proposed changes of the latter type with FDA before implementation.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors planning trials that could potentially be terminated early for efficacy reasons should discuss early stopping rules and boundaries with FDA prior to implementing the trial.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2.1 - DMC Recommendations to Terminate the Study
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors of trials that could potentially be terminated early for efficacy reasons discuss these issues with FDA prior to implementing the trial, when the statistical monitoring plan and early stopping boundaries are being developed.

rules.rule_text: The DMC must document its recommendations and the rationale for such recommendations in a form that can be reviewed by the sponsor and then circulated, as appropriate, to IRBs, FDA, and other interested parties.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.3.1 - Making Recommendations
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that a DMC document its recommendations, and the rationale for such recommendations, in a form that can be reviewed by the sponsor and then circulated, if and as appropriate, to IRBs, FDA, and/or other interested parties.

rules.rule_text: When the statistician responsible for interim analysis is employed by the sponsor, special care must be taken to ensure that confidential interim data are not revealed to anyone else within the sponsor organization.
rules.source_section: Section 6.4 - Statisticians Conducting the Interim Analyses
rules.verbatim_quote: If the statistician responsible for interim analysis and reporting to the DMC is employed by the sponsor, special care should be taken to minimize the potential for bias, such as ensuring that confidential interim data are not revealed to anyone else within the sponsor organization (see 21 CFR 314.126(b)(5) (drugs) and 21 CFR 860.7(f)(1) (devices)).

rules.rule_text: Investigators are responsible for assuring that IRBs are made aware of significant new information that arises about a clinical trial, including DMC recommendations communicated to the sponsor.
rules.source_section: Section 3.1 - Institutional Review Boards
rules.verbatim_quote: Under 21 CFR 56.103, 21 CFR 312.66, 21 CFR 812.40, and 21 CFR 812.150(a), individual investigators (or the sponsor of investigational devices) are responsible for assuring that IRBs are made aware of significant new information that arises about a clinical trial.

rules.rule_text: The DMC must be informed in a timely manner of any cases for which unblinding of treatment code at the clinical site or by the treating clinician is thought to be necessary to provide an appropriate intervention.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.1.2 - Monitoring for Safety
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that the DMC be informed in a timely manner of any cases for which unblinding of treatment code at the clinical site or by the treating clinician is thought to be necessary to provide an appropriate intervention, so that the DMC can assess the potential impact of such actions on the overall study blind.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor is responsible for monitoring and analyzing investigator adverse experience reports and relaying them to FDA, other regulatory authorities, and other investigators as required.
rules.source_section: Section 3.5 - Others with Monitoring Responsibilities
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor is responsible for monitoring and analyzing these investigator reports and relaying them as required to FDA, other regulatory authorities (as appropriate) and other investigators (21 CFR 312.32(c), 21 CFR 812.40).

rules.rule_text: In settings where a sponsor permits representatives or other non-DMC members to attend closed sessions of DMC meetings, the DMC must have the option of conducting an executive session with no participants other than DMC members.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3.1.2 - Meeting Structure
rules.verbatim_quote: In settings in which a sponsor chooses to permit its representatives or other non-DMC members to attend the closed session despite the risks of such arrangements, we recommend that the DMC have the option of conducting an 'executive' session with no participants other than DMC members.

rules.rule_text: The DMC should generally be provided with interim summaries by treatment arm of all adverse events observed, not limited to those identified in advance, particularly for serious events.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.1.2 - Monitoring for Safety
rules.verbatim_quote: Because many types of adverse reactions cannot be anticipated prior to a large-scale study, the DMC should generally be provided with interim summaries by treatment arm of adverse events observed, not limited to those identified in advance. This is particularly important for serious events that may result from the disease being treated as well as the intervention itself.

rules.rule_text: The DMC should have access to the actual treatment assignments for each study group in order to evaluate the balance of risks and benefits of the active interventions.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3.1.4 - Format of Interim Reports to the DMC and Use of Treatment Codes
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that a DMC have access to the actual treatment assignments for each study group.
rules.exceptions: A common approach is presentation of results in printed copy using codes (for example, Group A and Group B) to protect against inadvertent unblinding should a report be misplaced, with separate access to the actual study arm assignments provided to DMC members by the statistical group.

rules.rule_text: A process must be established to unblind treatment codes to DMC members in a timely fashion when needed to address an emerging safety concern rapidly.
rules.source_section: Section 4.3.1.4 - Format of Interim Reports to the DMC and Use of Treatment Codes
rules.verbatim_quote: To ensure the DMC's and sponsor's ability to address an emerging safety concern rapidly, they should establish a process to unblind treatment codes to DMC members in a timely fashion when needed (cf. 21 CFR 312.32(d); 21 CFR 812.46(b)(2)).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should contact FDA before initiating communication with the DMC regarding access to interim data from a trial likely to be an important part of a regulatory submission.
rules.source_section: Section 7.2 - Accessing Interim Data
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that sponsors contact FDA before initiating communication with the DMC regarding access to interim data from a trial likely to be an important part of a regulatory submission.
rules.exceptions: While FDA permission is not required, a discussion regarding the potential risks and implications of that action and of methods to limit the risks may contribute to informed decision making.
